event.message: 正在请求 AI 规划

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it as a compact visible-content index to disambiguate generic titles and sanitized URLs. Keep grouping fine-grained; do not merge broad workflows just because sampled pages are all readable.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"ai_coding_agents","title":"AI Coding Agents","color":"purple","confidence":0.81,"ids":[10000,10008],"reason":"Agent development and automated code review benchmarking pages."},{"key":"chrome_extension_apis","title":"Chrome Extension APIs","color":"blue","confidence":0.87,"ids":[10001,10018],"reason":"Chrome extension API references for side panel, tabs, and tab groups."},{"key":"llm_evaluation_research","title":"LLM Evaluation Research","color":"green","confidence":0.92,"ids":[10002,10009,10019],"reason":"Leaderboard, benchmark paper, and dataset discussion for LLM evaluation research."},{"key":"cloudflare_gateway_ops","title":"Cloudflare Gateway Ops","color":"cyan","confidence":0.84,"ids":[10003,10013],"reason":"Cloudflare Worker routing and tunnel diagnostics for gateway/infrastructure work."},{"key":"frontend_implementation","title":"Frontend Implementation","color":"yellow","confidence":0.86,"ids":[10004,10014,10021],"reason":"UI implementation, testing, and state-management debugging for frontend work."},{"key":"database_sql","title":"Database and SQL","color":"grey","confidence":0.72,"ids":[10005,10022],"reason":"Database migration and general database notes appear to support the same data workstream."},{"key":"video_media_queue","title":"Video / Media Queue","color":"red","confidence":0.82,"ids":[10006,10016],"reason":"Queued video or transcript content to watch or review later."},{"key":"product_ui_design","title":"Product UI Design","color":"pink","confidence":0.88,"ids":[10010,10012,10020],"reason":"Design references for settings patterns, side panel layouts, and visual UI examples."},{"key":"read_later","title":"Read Later","color":"grey","confidence":0.85,"ids":[10015,10023],"reason":"Background reading and reference material saved for later consumption."}],"review":[{"id":10007,"reason":"Shopping/finance page is sensitive and does not clearly belong to a broader work group."},{"id":10011,"reason":"Security checklist is too generic and its exact task context is unclear."},{"id":10017,"reason":"Subscription pricing review appears finance-related and sensitive; keep for manual review."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Planning JSON output**

I need to create a compact JSON output plan, ensuring each tab is represented exactly once in groups for review. Choosing English as the likely language from titles is important. I'll aim for fine-grained semantic groups using adjacency, and while I won’t exceed 40 groups, that shouldn’t be an issue. It’s possible to group tabs by topic across windows, even if they’re non-contiguous, while maintaining the first tab sequence. I'll apply a conservative approach with a minimum confidence of .65. Sensitive shopping and finance data might need extra review.

event.message: 已找到 9 个主题方向

messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it as a compact visible-content index to disambiguate generic titles and sanitized URLs. Keep grouping fine-grained; do not merge broad workflows just because sampled pages are all readable.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.
Refine this coarse bucket: 待分类.
Coarse reason: 粗分阶段认为这些标签页仍不确定。
This is a large-session refinement pass; prefer concise semantic clustering and strict JSON over deep reasoning.
Split it only when there are clearly different semantic tasks or topics.
Keep uncertain or sensitive tabs in reviewTabs.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"purchase_comparison","title":"Purchase Comparison","color":"blue","confidence":0.93,"ids":[10007,10034],"reason":"Both tabs are shopping/finance pages focused on purchase comparison and checkout evaluation."},{"key":"pricing_review","title":"Pricing Review","color":"green","confidence":0.9,"ids":[10017,10044],"reason":"Both tabs are pricing-related finance pages for reviewing subscription or product pricing."},{"key":"billing_invoices","title":"Billing & Invoices","color":"yellow","confidence":0.88,"ids":[10036],"reason":"This tab is specifically about invoice and billing management, distinct from comparison or pricing review."}],"review":[]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing JSON data**

I need to output only in JSON and classify all tabs exactly once. The language seems to be English based on the titles. I'll group them by their semantic task or topic, and keep the order and context in mind. For shopping and finance tabs, I could compare purchases 10007 and 10034 together since they're noncontiguous. Subscription pricing review for 10017 and pricing page 10044 could also be grouped. I'll keep invoice billing 10036 separate. My confidence in this approach is moderate, and I’ll ensure concise reasoning.
